_doc:
Phase-5d demo: RAM with sync write, async read, and pre-loaded contents. Click VERILOG to see the full memory exported:
  • `reg [7:0] mem_<id> [0:15]` — 16-deep × 8-bit Verilog memory array.
  • `assign rd = re ? mem[addr] : 8'h0;` — RE-gated async read. Asserting RE=1 returns the indexed cell; RE=0 returns 0 (matches the engine's hold-on-low semantics best-effort).
  • `always @(posedge clk) if (we) mem[addr] <= data;` — synchronous write port.
  • `initial begin mem[0] = 8'h12; mem[1] = 8'h34; ...` — pre-load. Three cells (0/1/2) are seeded with values 0x12 / 0x34 / 0x56. iverilog and FPGA toolchains honour the initial; ASIC users would replace it with $readmemh or a reset path.
